build_system.build_command: cd pyscf/lib && mkdir -p build && cd build && cmake .. && cmake --build . -j4

configuration_parameters.description: 2S spin multiplicity parameter used by PySCF; allyl radical uses spin=1.
configuration_parameters.name: spin

configuration_parameters.description: Number of excited states requested; maps to nroots.
configuration_parameters.name: nstates

configuration_parameters.description: Restricted spin channel selector; RKS/RHF singlet cases use true, unrestricted solvers use null.
configuration_parameters.name: singlet

configuration_parameters.description: Frozen orbital specification passed to TD factory.
configuration_parameters.name: frozen

configuration_parameters.description: TD residual convergence tolerance; must not be loosened.
configuration_parameters.name: conv_tol

configuration_parameters.description: Memory limit in MB; _lr_eig derives max_space from this.
configuration_parameters.name: max_memory

configuration_parameters.description: Deterministic DFT grid settings used by tests and requested goal.
configuration_parameters.name: dft_grid_settings

entry_points.call_signature: mol = gto.M(...); mf = dft.RKS(mol) | dft.UKS(mol) | scf.RHF(mol) | scf.UHF(mol); mf.kernel()
entry_points.description: Builds molecule and converged orbital/occupation data consumed by TDDFT/TDA response operators.
entry_points.name: Ground-state SCF preparation

entry_points.call_signature: tdscf.TDA(mf, frozen=None); tdscf.TDDFT(mf, frozen=None); mf.TDA(); mf.TDDFT(); mf.CasidaTDDFT()
entry_points.description: Public API for constructing TD solvers. DFT TDDFT dispatches to hybrid TDDFT or no-hybrid CasidaTDDFT; HF TDDFT dispatches to TDHF.
entry_points.name: TD object factories

entry_points.description: Runs TDA diagonalization through gen_vind(), get_precond(), and pyscf.tdscf._lr_eig.eigh(). RKS/UKS inherit these paths with DFT response functions.
entry_points.name: TDA kernels

entry_points.description: Runs full linear-response eigenproblem. Real molecular systems use _lr_eig.real_eig(); complex systems use _lr_eig.eig().
entry_points.name: Hybrid TDHF/TDDFT kernels

entry_points.description: Runs squared Casida equation via _lr_eig.eigh(), then converts w^2 roots to excitation energies and X/Y amplitudes.
entry_points.name: No-hybrid Casida TDDFT kernels

entry_points.call_signature: eigh(aop, x0, precond, tol_residual=1e-5, lindep=1e-12, nroots=1, x0sym=None, pick=None, max_cycle=50, max_memory=MAX_MEMORY, verbose=logger.WARN); eig(...); real_eig(...)
entry_points.description: Target Davidson-style subspace solvers. They batch response applications, build projected subspace matrices, diagonalize reduced problems, precondition residuals, and return convergence flags, eigenvalues, and eigenvectors.
entry_points.name: Linear-response eigensolvers

output_quantities.description: Excitation energies in Hartree, ordered by requested positive roots.
Boolean convergence status per requested root; all requested roots should converge.
Oscillator strengths for closed-shell singlet cases where exposed by the benchmark.
Transition dipole vectors; compare sign-invariant norms or allow global sign flips per state.
Wall time around TD kernel only, after SCF preparation; primary optimization metric is weighted median across cases.
Number of Davidson/subspace outer iterations if instrumented from _lr_eig debug loop.
Number of response operator calls aop/vind if instrumented; secondary performance metric.

runtime_command_template: import json, os, time; import numpy as np; from pyscf import gto, dft, scf, tdscf; p=json.loads(os.environ['CASE_JSON']); dft.radi.ATOM_SPECIFIC_TREUTLER_GRIDS=False; mol=gto.M(atom=p['atom'], basis=p['basis'], charge=p.get('charge',0), spin=p.get('spin',0), symmetry=p.get('symmetry',False), verbose=0); mf=(getattr(dft,p['scf_method'])(mol) if p['scf_method'] in ('RKS','UKS') else getattr(scf,p['scf_method'])(mol)); mf.xc=p.get('xc', getattr(mf,'xc',None)) if hasattr(mf,'xc') else None; mf.grids.prune=None if hasattr(mf,'grids') else None; mf.kernel(conv_tol=p.get('scf_conv_tol',1e-10)); td=getattr(mf,p['td_method'])(p.get('frozen',None)); td.nstates=p['nstates']; td.conv_tol=p.get('td_conv_tol',td.conv_tol); t0=time.perf_counter(); e,xy=td.kernel(); dt=time.perf_counter()-t0; osc=td.oscillator_strength().tolist() if p.get('oscillator_strength',False) else None; print(json.dumps({'e':np.asarray(e).tolist(),'converged':np.asarray(td.converged,dtype=bool).tolist(),'oscillator_strength':osc,'td_kernel_wall_time_s':dt}))

suggested_cases.description: Closed-shell no-hybrid RKS Casida TDDFT benzene training case.
Closed-shell hybrid RKS full TDDFT benzene training case.
Open-shell no-hybrid UKS Casida TDDFT allyl radical training case.
Larger closed-shell no-hybrid RKS Casida TDDFT benzene test case.
Larger closed-shell hybrid RKS full TDDFT benzene test case.
Larger open-shell no-hybrid UKS Casida TDDFT allyl radical test case.

threading_model.description: Python control flow is serial. Heavy response, integral, XC, NumPy, and SciPy operations use compiled PySCF C extensions, OpenMP when enabled by CMake, and linked BLAS/LAPACK threading. PySCF exposes pyscf.lib.num_threads(n) for OpenMP runtime control. Benchmark runs should pin OpenMP and BLAS thread counts to 1 to avoid noise.
